data.content:
Yes, C# fully supports `stackalloc`'d `Span` of native-sized integers. Both `nint` and `nuint` work:

```csharp
Span<nint>  span  = stackalloc nint[4];   // ✅ works
Span<nuint> uspan = stackalloc nuint[3];  // ✅ works
```

Under the hood, `nint` maps to `IntPtr` and `nuint` to `UIntPtr`. The size of each element is pointer-sized (8 bytes on 64-bit, 4 bytes on 32-bit). This has been supported since C# 9 / .NET 5, when `nint`/`nuint` were introduced as first-class numeric types.
